data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: Apple's website is powered by a robust, multi-layered infrastructure that allows it to handle high traffic volumes efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Cloud-based architecture using AWS services with autoscaling capabilities.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: The website employs a global CDN to deliver content quickly to users around the world.
data.technical_spec: Uses Akamai CDN for efficient content delivery and reduced latency.

data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Apple.com uses a microservices architecture to decouple services for better manageability and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Containerized services orchestrated through Kubernetes clusters.

data.title: API-driven Design
data.description: The site heavily relies on APIs for data interchange between various components and services.
data.technical_spec: RESTful API endpoints secured with OAuth 2.0.

data.title: Edge Computing
data.description: Leverages edge computing for processing data closer to users to ensure faster response times.
data.technical_spec: Edge nodes powered by edge cloud providers for compute-intensive tasks.
